perturbation.original_text: 5. Breach. The Recipient acknowledges that disclosure or use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement could cause irreparable harm to Discloser including loss of lives and limbs of the persons and damage to the property, for which monetary damages may be difficult to ascertain or turn to be meaningless. The Recipient therefore agrees that Discloser will have the right, in addition to its other rights and remedies, to seek injunctive relief for violations of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Breach. The Recipient acknowledges that disclosure or use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may not cause irreparable harm to Discloser and that monetary damages are readily ascertainable and adequate compensation. The Recipient therefore agrees that Discloser will not have the right to seek injunctive relief for violations of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause asserts potential irreparable harm and the right to injunctive relief. The modified version denies irreparable harm and removes the right to injunctive relief, directly contradicting the initial assertions within the same section. This casts doubt on the remedies available to the Discloser in case of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Breach. The Recipient acknowledges that disclosure or use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may not cause irreparable harm to Discloser and that monetary damages are readily ascertainable and adequate compensation. The Recipient therefore agrees that Discloser will not have the right to seek injunctive relief for violations of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5 directly contradicts itself. The original clause asserts irreparable harm and the right to injunctive relief, while the modified version denies irreparable harm and removes the right to injunctive relief. This direct conflict within the same section constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, as the changed text is identical to the contradicted text, creating a structural flaw in the contract as described in the definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2.  Confidentiality: 2.1 No Use: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any way or under any circumstances share the same, in writing or through any other means, with any Third Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.  Confidentiality: 2.1 No Use: Recipient agrees to use the Confidential Information to improve the CCTV system and may share the information to subcontractors to further improve the CCTV system.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits the Recipient from using or sharing the Confidential Information. The modified version permits the Recipient to use the information for improvement purposes and share it with subcontractors. This direct contradiction within the same section creates confusion about the permitted use of the Confidential Information, essentially nullifying the confidentiality provision's intended purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.  Confidentiality: 2.1 No Use: Recipient agrees to use the Confidential Information to improve the CCTV system and may share the information to subcontractors to further improve the CCTV system.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2.1 prohibits any use or sharing of Confidential Information. The changed text in Section 2.1 permits the use of Confidential Information for specific improvement purposes and allows sharing with subcontractors. The contradicted text, located at the same spot as the changed text, also permits use and sharing. However, the key is the original text that the change is based on - which created the contradiction. The original was a strict NO USE clause and therefore this qualifies as a strong, direct contradiction as it completely reverses the restriction, thus qualifying for a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Miscellaneous. a) Except in the event of an amalgamation or merger with or takeover by a third party of their business, neither Party may assign or transfer its rights or obligations in this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Miscellaneous. a) Either Party may assign or transfer its rights or obligations in this Agreement to any affiliate or subsidiary company, without the prior written consent of the other. The terms of assignment are at the sole discretion of the assigning party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause restricts assignment or transfer of rights unless through merger/takeover, and even then requires consent. The modification allows assignment to affiliates or subsidiaries without consent, directly contradicting the original restriction within the same section. The added discretion in assignment terms further undermines the original control over transfer of obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Miscellaneous. a) Either Party may assign or transfer its rights or obligations in this Agreement to any affiliate or subsidiary company, without the prior written consent of the other. The terms of assignment are at the sole discretion of the assigning party.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires consent for assignment except in cases of merger/takeover. The changed text allows assignment to affiliates/subsidiaries *without* consent. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. The changed text introduces a direct exception to the consent requirement, creating a clear contradiction with the original condition of requiring consent. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the clause is in the same location with conflicting implications, and thus it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this Non- Discloser Agreement shall be setteled amicably by the Parties through mutual negotiations. Any unsettled dispute or difference shall be referred to Arbitration by a Sole Arbitrator. The Arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with the rules and procedure of UNCITRAL in force on the date of Agreement. Arbitration proceedings shall be held in India and will be conducted in English. The decision of Arbitral Tribunal shall be final and binding on all Parties. Cost of Arbitrtaion shall be borne by Parties themselves unless and otherwise ordered by the Tribunal.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this Non- Discloser Agreement shall be settled amicably by the Parties through mutual negotiations. If amicable settlement fails, all disputes will be resolved through litigation in the courts of [Recipient's Country]. The prevailing party shall bear all legal costs.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates arbitration for dispute resolution. The modification replaces this with litigation in the courts of the Recipient's Country, contradicting the arbitration clause within the same section. This creates a conflict about the dispute resolution mechanism.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this Non- Discloser Agreement shall be settled amicably by the Parties through mutual negotiations. If amicable settlement fails, all disputes will be resolved through litigation in the courts of [Recipient's Country]. The prevailing party shall bear all legal costs.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies arbitration for dispute resolution, while the changed text mandates litigation in the courts of the Recipient's Country, creating a direct contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, highlighting that the change introduces a conflict within the contract regarding the method for resolving disputes. This adheres to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, where a clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, the same location but with conflicting implications after the change).
